## Additional fields bug

If a user specifies an "additional_fields" kwarg to VaspToDb that's MSONable, fireworks serializes and deserializes the object, but the vasp drone doesn't, so when it's inserted into mongo or the json output, it's cast to a string, rather than an as_dict representation of the object.  This PR fixes by using jsanitize to recursively cast "additional_fields" to json at the beginning of the VASP output analysis.

pr_title: Fix a regression in determining when to collect static files.
pr_body:
A commit made in 1.6.10 (ae6b3f8eaedffaf09860386c69763ead5a43c94d)
changed a storage lookup to use the prefixed_path variable instead of
the path variable when looking up modification times in order to
determine if an existing collected file should be deleted and
re-collected. By using the wrong path variable, the storage lookups
always failed, resulting in every single file being re-collected every
single time.

This reduces performance considerably, particularly with large codebases.
Since every file ends up copied, they appear as new and are recompiled.
This can result in very long page reloads on a development server,
especially if working on an older machine, in a VM, on a shared
filesystem, or on the Linux subsystem for Windows.

This is a simple change that fixes the lookup to use the correct
variable. Unit tests were added to ensure this continues to work as
expected and does not regress.

## Problem

This fixes #274, which is a regression on Django 1.9/1.10 causing many unnecessary database queries, because the ``_get_sub_obj_recurse`` logic is run twice (once on the parent instance and again on the subclass instance). The cached values from the ``select_related(...)`` call in ``select_subclasses()`` get lost when selecting the subclass instances, and the database is hit again, _for ~ each item in the queryset!_

## Solution

On Django >= 1.9, ``InheritanceQuerySetMixin.iterator()`` now yields from ``InheritanceIterable`` and doesn't re-run the ``_get_sub_obj_recurse`` logic.

## Commandments

- [x] Write PEP8 compliant code.
- [x] Cover it with tests.
- [x] Update `CHANGES.rst` file to describe the changes, and quote according issue with `GH-<issue_number>`.
- [x] Pay attention to backward compatibility, or if it breaks it, explain why.
- [ ] Update documentation (if relevant).

`Mle`: Ensure to send/queue the "Child Update Response" before the "Data Request"
pr_body:
This commit adds two fixes in `Mle::HandleChildUpdateRequest()`:

- "Child Update Response" is queued/sent before the "Data Request".
   This change addresses the issue during parent reset recovery where
   (without this change) the parent  would receive the "Data Request"
   before the "Child Update Response" and would ignore it (as the child
   is still being restored).

- The `tlv length` parameter in call to `SendDataRequest()` is fixed.
  This change addresses the issue where incorrect (number of) TLVs
  were being requested/appended to the "Data Request" message.

@ethanbao I was going to do some UX hacking on Artman, and discovered that it does not support Python 3. This pull request fixes that.

It does the following:

  * Updates the code to support Python 2.7 and 3.5. This mostly means `print` is consistently used as a function now, and calls to `urllib`, `urlparse`, and `urllib2` now all pull from `six.moves.urllib`, which mirrors the Python 3 package.
  * Substantially refactors the test running. Running tox now does the following, in order:
      * Runs the Python 2.7 tests.
      * Runs the Python 3.5 tests.
      * Runs the linter (on Python 3.5).
      * Puts the coverage data together and produces a report.
  * The `.travis.yml` is also changed to reflect this.

pr_title: bug 1315415 - use imageArtifactHash to verify built docker image hashes
pr_body:
Previously, we had no way of matching up built docker image hashes and the docker image used:

- docker-image build would report the sha of the tarball
- build would report the sha of the docker image built from the tarball

with https://github.com/taskcluster/docker-worker/pull/262 (which is live as of earlier this week), the build also reports the sha of the downloaded image tarball as `imageArtifactHash`, which we can compare to the sha from the docker-image build.  Thanks @gregarndt!

This PR adds a new `zmq.web.zmqweb` module.  This module adds the ability to run `tornado.web.RequestHandler`s in a scalable and distributed manner using zmq ROUTER/DEALER sockets.  You can instantiate multiple instances of handlers in backend processes and requests to those handlers will be load balanced.  See the example in `examples/zmqweb` for a simple demonstration.  The core work is done but there are some additional things that I need to finish before this is merged:

* Better example and documentation.
* Testing.
